rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday March 7, 2018 line 3. PLAINTIFF PAUL VANDER WAERDT MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD GRANTED. No opposition filed. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFERSON CHRISMAN VS. HOWARD LEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday March 7, 2018 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFERSON CHRISMAN MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT GRANTED with leave to amend to allege facts giving rise to contract-based attorney's fees. Cross-Complainant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that statutory attorneys fees under SFRO are applicable to non-SFRO causes of action alleged by the cross-complainant in the cross-complaint. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFERSON CHRISMAN VS. HOWARD LEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday March 7, 2018 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFERSON CHRISMAN DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OVERRULED. Cross-complaint sufficiently alleges causes of action for common count and interference with prospective economic relations. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN YUDOWITZ VS. MART BAILEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Form Interoggs. General (Set One) Form Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 7, 2018, Line 8. DEFENDANT MART BAILEY'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interoggs. General (Set One) Form Interoggs. Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on March 7, 2018: (1) Defendant to produce the envelope in which responses to discovery were received; (2) Plaintiff to produce corroborating proof of mailing or proof of payment for postage (receipt; tracking number; credit/debit card redacted statement, etc.) for service of discovery responses on the date indicated in the Proof of Service. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VLADISLAV CHERNOGUZ VS. SILVIA URRUTIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 7, 2018, Line 9. DEFENDANTS SILVIA URRUTIA, JUAN URRUTIA, JOSHUA URRUTIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, regarding habitual late payment. Factual allegation regarding late payment in August 2017 is not sufficient to support Plaintiff's conclusory assertion that Defendant's payments are habitually late. The Court makes no ruling regarding CCP 430.10(c) at this time as it is not clear from the face of the complaint what occasions of late payment are included in "previously noticed occasions." (See 60 Day Notice) =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: NELSON FAVENIR VS. ANTONIO GOMEZ ET AL
